description_long: This control cabinet is a rectangular, box-shaped unit with a robust, industrial appearance, primarily finished in a matte olive green color with a lighter beige front panel. The front face features multiple rotary knobs, switches, and analog meter displays, suggesting it is used for monitoring and controlling electrical or communication systems. Two prominent black dials and several toggle switches are visible, along with mesh-covered ventilation grilles for airflow. The sides and top are solid, unadorned metal panels, contributing to its sturdy, utilitarian design. The cabinet appears to be constructed from heavy-gauge metal, likely steel, with a painted finish for durability in outdoor or urban environments. Its size and features indicate it is intended for stationary installation, possibly in a utility area, street corner, or industrial site.
description: A robust, olive green and beige metal control cabinet with multiple dials, switches, analog meters, and ventilation grilles on the front. The sides and top are solid, unadorned metal panels, giving it a sturdy, industrial look.
description_view_0: The front view shows the control interface with dials, switches, analog meters, and ventilation grilles, all set on a beige panel framed by green.
description_view_1: The left side is a plain, solid green metal panel with no visible features except for the protruding ends of some front-facing controls.
description_view_2: The top view reveals a flat, unbroken green metal surface with no visible controls or openings.
description_view_3: The right side is another plain, solid green metal panel, mirroring the left side with no visible features.
